page.title: hepatitis B virus infection — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for hepatitis B virus infection: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for hepatitis B virus infection: 109 alterations and 363 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Chronic HBV: spontaneous HBsAg loss (functional cure) in 0.5-2%/year on antiviral therapy, 0.1-0.5%/year without treatment; HBeAg seroconversion spontaneous in 5-10%/year; progression to cirrhosis in 20-25% of untreated chronic HBV over 20 years
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Current antivirals (entecavir, tenofovir): suppress HBV DNA to undetectable in >95% but do not clear cccDNA reservoir; functional cure (HBsAg loss) in 1-3%/year on antiviral; combination anti-HBsAg approaches in trials showing 20-40% functional cure rates at 48 weeks
remission.gap_size: Very large — suppressive therapy prevents cirrhosis but does not cure; functional cure rate on current therapy is very low; treatment gap in LMIC enormous (only 30% of eligible patients treated globally)
remission.barrier_detail: The cccDNA reservoir in hepatocytes cannot be cleared by current antivirals; cure requires either HBsAg clearance or cccDNA elimination; 296 million people with chronic HBV globally, only ~30% on treatment; HBV vaccination has transformed prevention but treatment of existing infection remains suboptimal
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — AASLD and EASL guidelines both shifted toward expanding treatment eligibility regardless of ALT or fibrosis stage; tenofovir alafenamide (TAF) replaced TDF as preferred tenofovir form 2016; entecavir and TDF as first-line since 2005-2008; no paradigm change for >15 years
remission.outdated_treatments: Lamivudine monotherapy (high resistance rate — 70% at 5 years; replaced by entecavir/tenofovir); conventional interferon-alfa (replaced by PEG-IFN; more side effects, lower efficacy); sequential monotherapy (resistance development) vs immediate high-barrier-to-resistance agents
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Lifestyle modification (alcohol cessation critical; reduces cirrhosis and HCC risk even on antiviral); surveillance protocols for HCC (underperformed globally); coffee consumption (3+ cups/day: 40% HCC risk reduction in observational data)
remission.frontier_signal: RNA interference therapy (vir-2218, RG6346 — HBsAg knockdown enabling immune reconstitution; Phase 2 positive); capsid assembly modulators (NVR 3-778, ABI-H0731); HBsAg-targeting antibodies; therapeutic vaccines (HepTcell, GS-4774); CRISPR targeting cccDNA in preclinical models
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
